---
title: "Student notebooks"
---

Run these documents in **RStudio** or **Positron** (or render with Quarto). The site uses **two parallel tracks**: **simulated genes** (we designed the data so we know the truth) and **Palmer Penguins** (one real dataset all week). See the **[data catalog](../data/index.qmd)** and **[Palmer Penguins data card](../data/cards/palmer-penguins.qmd)** for context.

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
## Notebooks vs modules

- **These `.Rmd` files** are **long labs** (**Lab_exploration**; homework when your instructor assigns them): lots of chunks, plots, and “change this and re-run.”
- **Modules** ([`modules/`](../modules/index.qmd)) are shorter **website** pages: **concepts** (01–03, 05–06) plus **canonical `tidymodels` code** on penguins (**04** — train/test + Day 2 include — and **07–08**). Day 2 (Tuesday) slides share the **same include** as [Module 04](../modules/module-04-pipeline.qmd).

**Notebook inventory**

| Notebook | Primary role | Companion module(s) | Download |
|----------|----------------|----------------------|----------|
| [linear-regression-lasso.Rmd](linear-regression-lasso.Rmd) | Lab_exploration | [Module 02](../modules/module-02-regularization.qmd) (concepts) | [Download](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/s-peischl/ML_SS_2026/main/notebooks/linear-regression-lasso.Rmd) |
| [logistic-regression-gene-disease.Rmd](logistic-regression-gene-disease.Rmd) | Lab_exploration | [Module 01](../modules/module-01-foundations.qmd), [Module 03](../modules/module-03-trees-and-cultures.qmd) | [Download](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/s-peischl/ML_SS_2026/main/notebooks/logistic-regression-gene-disease.Rmd) |
| [penguins-body-mass.Rmd](penguins-body-mass.Rmd) | Lab_exploration | [Module 02](../modules/module-02-regularization.qmd) | [Download](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/s-peischl/ML_SS_2026/main/notebooks/penguins-body-mass.Rmd) |
| [penguins-classification.Rmd](penguins-classification.Rmd) | Lab_exploration | [Module 03](../modules/module-03-trees-and-cultures.qmd); **`tidymodels`** path: [04 train/test](../modules/module-04-pipeline.qmd#train-test-last-fit), [04 pipeline](../modules/module-04-pipeline.qmd#canonical-pipeline-tuesday), [07](../modules/module-07-penguins-choose-metrics.qmd), [08](../modules/module-08-penguins-compare-models.qmd) | [Download](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/s-peischl/ML_SS_2026/main/notebooks/penguins-classification.Rmd) |
| [penguins-sex-classification.Rmd](penguins-sex-classification.Rmd) | Lab_exploration | [Module 01](../modules/module-01-foundations.qmd) (splits / leakage ideas) | [Download](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/s-peischl/ML_SS_2026/main/notebooks/penguins-sex-classification.Rmd) |
| [penguins-species-multiclass.Rmd](penguins-species-multiclass.Rmd) | Lab_exploration | [Module 06](../modules/module-06-evaluation-and-interpretability.qmd) | [Download](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/s-peischl/ML_SS_2026/main/notebooks/penguins-species-multiclass.Rmd) |
| [penguins-multiclass-upsampling.Rmd](penguins-multiclass-upsampling.Rmd) | Minimal lab | [Day 4 Part E](../slides/day-04-thursday.html#/part-e-metrics) | [Download](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/s-peischl/ML_SS_2026/main/notebooks/penguins-multiclass-upsampling.Rmd) |
| [penguins-shap.Rmd](penguins-shap.Rmd) | Lab_exploration | [Module 06 SHAP](../modules/module-06-evaluation-and-interpretability.qmd#shap-shapley-values-on-penguins-sex) | [Download](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/s-peischl/ML_SS_2026/main/notebooks/penguins-shap.Rmd) |
| [penguins-pca.Rmd](penguins-pca.Rmd) | Lab_exploration | [Day 4 PCA block](../slides/day-04-thursday.html#/part-c-recipes) | [Download](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/s-peischl/ML_SS_2026/main/notebooks/penguins-pca.Rmd) |
:::

::: {.hub-muted}
Tip: use **Render** in the IDE, or from the project root run `quarto render notebooks/your-file.Rmd`.
:::

## Synthetic genes (we know the answers) {#synthetic-genes}

Ideal for **LASSO, ridge, AIC**, and logistic regression when we **know** which simulated features should matter.

- [linear-regression-lasso.Rmd](linear-regression-lasso.Rmd) — gene–trait simulation, plots, **penalized regression** (`glmnet` package), forward/backward model choice by AIC (`MASS::stepAIC` in the code).
- [logistic-regression-gene-disease.Rmd](logistic-regression-gene-disease.Rmd) — simulated gene expression and disease status, `glm` logistic regression, `rpart` tree, confusion table, decision-boundary plot.

## Palmer Penguins (continuity) {#palmer-penguins-continuity}

Same real table as on the **[Palmer Penguins data card](../data/cards/palmer-penguins.qmd)**. **Models:** **`lm` / `glm` / `rpart` / `glmnet`**. **Splits, scaling, and metrics:** **`tidymodels`** (`rsample`, `recipes`, `yardstick`).

For the **Day 2 (Tuesday) `tidymodels` pipeline** (recipe + workflow + cross-validation / tuning on the same Adelie vs Gentoo task), use **[Module 04](../modules/module-04-pipeline.qmd#train-test-last-fit)** (starts with **train/test + `glm`**) and **[Module 04 — canonical pipeline](../modules/module-04-pipeline.qmd#canonical-pipeline-tuesday)** (shared tuned-tree walk-through with Day 2 slides), then **[Module 07](../modules/module-07-penguins-choose-metrics.qmd)** (accuracy vs kappa vs AUC while tuning) and **[Module 08](../modules/module-08-penguins-compare-models.qmd)** (RF vs XGBoost vs small neural net).

- [penguins-body-mass.Rmd](penguins-body-mass.Rmd) — plots and correlations, then ridge/LASSO to predict **body mass** (code uses `glmnet` like the gene notebook).  
- [penguins-classification.Rmd](penguins-classification.Rmd) — **Adelie vs Gentoo**: logistic regression and a tree (`rpart`), who-was-misclassified table, simple 2D decision picture.  
- [penguins-sex-classification.Rmd](penguins-sex-classification.Rmd) — **female vs male** from size measures (**species left out on purpose**), train/test split, same two model types.  
- [penguins-species-multiclass.Rmd](penguins-species-multiclass.Rmd) — **three species**: multinomial logistic regression (`nnet::multinom`) and a multiclass tree, confusion tables and a heatmap.  
- [penguins-multiclass-upsampling.Rmd](penguins-multiclass-upsampling.Rmd) — **rare Chinstrap** + one tree model: compare confusion matrices with and without `step_upsample()`.
- [penguins-shap.Rmd](penguins-shap.Rmd) — SHAP notebook: beeswarm with individual points, mean \|SHAP\| ranking, and waterfall plots for individual penguins.
- [penguins-pca.Rmd](penguins-pca.Rmd) — PCA notebook: PCA biplot/loadings and compact with-vs-without PCA model comparison.

## Requirements

Install as needed (copy–paste into R): `palmerpenguins`, `tidyr`, `nnet`, `glmnet`, `tidymodels`, `dplyr`, `GGally`, `ggplot2`, `knitr`, `MASS`, `rpart`, `rpart.plot`, `ranger`, `xgboost`, `themis` (Day 4 Thursday slides), `rcartocolor` (optional colours in the gene linear notebook).
